“I think the whole premise of it is when you find out that you find a Traitor, you are supposed to murder them and get them out,” he explains. He says his plan was to keep her around and see if she would help him eliminate another Traitor.
“I think it was an interesting word to use,” Lisa replies.
Candiace agrees. “Maybe the wrong word,” she chimes in.
Colton says Lisa also said in the turret she wanted to “slit my f—- throat.”
Lisa acknowledges she said that. “I can look at you any way I want and I can say whatever I want, actually,” she adds.
Colton then addresses the overwhelming criticism he has received since the show began.

“I think what I can understand from people viewing me back on television, if you didn’t follow my story and what I went through and you watched me as the ‘Bachelor’ in 2019 and then all of a sudden you’re tuning in in 2026 — I had an incredibly difficult couple of years,” Colton says. “I’ve grown and I’ve changed and I’ve put so much work into myself and into my family. I never really thought the way that I would show up and play ‘Traitors’ would be linked to one of the most darkest and most horrible moments of my life.”
He said he can understand why people are talking about it.
Porsha Williams and Michael Rapaport Go Head-to-Head
Michael Rapaport defends his chaotic gameplay. He says he knew Porsha Williams wasn’t a Traitor but he continued to push for her to go because his “integrity was on the line.”
Porsha replies, “Michael, you did not have any integrity in that moment.” She adds that she feels like he used her.
“You need to figure out a way to use your words … when I’m talking, you’re not talking,” Michael screams as a back-and-forth begins.
Finally, Andy has to step in to shut down the argument.
Michael Speaks About His Colton Round Table Comment

Andy brings up the controversial comment Michael made at a Round Table about Colton being able to keep a secret, which led some viewers to believe it was a reference to the former “Bachelor” star’s sexuality.

“I feel really bad that it got to that point. I feel really bad that I antagonized Colton,” Michael says. “I reached out to him. As much as I like to talk trash, I’m not a person that likes to be respectful or hurt feelings.”
Colton says he had planned to keep the private conversation with Michael between them. He says anything that wasn’t game related “was off limits” for him.

title: 'Love Story': The True Story of JFK Jr. and Carolyn Bessette's Explosive Fight in a New York Park
summary: Why did JFK Jr and Carolyn Bessette get in a fight in a Manhattan park?
story: A new FX series series titled “Love Story” explores the highs and lows of John F. Kennedy Jr. and Carolyn Bessette's relationship.
The couple, who died in a plane crash in 1999 along with Bessette's sister Lauren Bessette, captivated the world with their whirlwind romance, which was covered extensively in tabloids at the time.
The fifth episode, titled "Battery Park," depicts the couple's alleged fight in a New York City park in 1996. What happened between them, and how was it covered in the media at the time?
Here's what to know.
Did JFK Jr. and Carolyn Bessette Really Fight at Battery Park?
In Bessette’s biography “Once Upon a Time,” which inspired the FX series, author Elizabeth Beller offers insight into the fight that caused a stir in the tabloids.
Per Beller, Bessette and Kennedy did indeed have a very public argument in February 1996. The disagreement occurred after the couple had brunch at the Tribeca Grill, per Beller's book.
“First, they argued in front of the restaurant, both making wild gestures and obviously screaming at each other. John’s face was full of fury, and Carolyn gave it right back to him,” she wrote.
Publications at the time reported that the couple fought in Battery Park, but Beller’s book shares a different description.

“They went home to get their dog, Friday, and completely unaware their every move was being captured on camera by paparazzo Angie Coqueran, brought him to Washington Square Park, where they continued their quarrel,” she wrote.
During the argument, Kennedy pulled Bessette’s engagement ring off of her finger.

“They were both crying, pushing each other, and yelling at the top of their lungs. John stormed off, and Carolyn ran after him, not one to stand for being left in the lurch. When she reached him, she leapt on his back. He turned around, shoved her off, and then they yelled more, close up in each other’s faces,” Beller wrote.
After stopping to sit on a park bench for a few minutes, the couple continued walking and “were at it again after a few blocks.”
“Finally, John just sat down on the curb, buried his head in his arms, and cried. Carolyn was pacing back and forth, and then offered her hand, and John put the ring back on,” Beller wrote.
What Were John and Carolyn Fighting About?

In the show, Carolyn and John fight over his remarks at the unveiling of George Magazine. He denied, both in a statement and brief remarks, that they were engaged. This sparks a blowout — both that John spoke about their private lives and that what he said was untrue.
The real cause, it seems, is different.
In her book, Beller notes that many news outlets incorrectly reported what caused the argument, saying it was about “Carolyn’s jealousy over John flirting with another woman.”
“Some reports had the other woman as Sharon Stone, some claimed it was O.J. Simpson’s girlfriend Paula Barbieri, and some didn’t name anyone specifically but noted John’s ‘roving eye,’” she wrote.
Beller explained that the fight was actually a result of “Carolyn’s anger that John was letting himself be exploited” and letting others take advantage of him.

Beller said Steve Gillon, one of Kennedy's friends, told her about an incident that had taken place at a wedding which caused conflict. Bessette felt the bride was using Kennedy's presence to get the New York Times to cover her wedding, and was upset he didn't "make a statement by walking out."
What Happened After the Fight?
Unbeknownst to the couple, their argument was captured on video.

“‘Fights, Camera, Action’ was the headline George Rush ran in the Daily News when he reported that a weekly television tabloid show called Day & Date, on CBS, had won the bidding war for the tape and would be broadcasting the entirety of the fight in living color over five days beginning on Monday, March 11,” Beller wrote.
The author also described how the National Enquirer's Steve Coz initially bought the tape and photos and told Newsweek the bidding war for the tape was "in the $100,000 range."
"Soon enough, more photos of the fight appeared. Each report was worse, more salacious, more vile than the last," Beller wrote.

Both Kennedy and Bessette faced backlash for their public fight, but Beller noted that Bessette got the brunt of the public's criticism. She was "captured ... in one of her worst moments," according to the book.
The coverage had ramifications on her life: Soon after, she left her job at Calvin Klein.

"It had become increasingly impossible for her to walk into the office due to the stalking paparazzi's presence. With the chaos of the press tracking her every move, perhaps even taking attention away from the brand, it no longer made sense to stay on," she wrote.
Bessette and Kennedy married in 1996 and died in a plane crash, along with Bessette's sister Lauren Bessette, in 1999.

title: 'Love Story': When Did JFK Jr. Propose and How Long Did it Take Carolyn Bessette to Say Yes?
summary: When did JFK Jr propose to Carolyn Bessette? How long did it take her to say yes? Why was she worried?
story: Episode 5 of “Love Story” reaches a pivotal turning point in John F. Kennedy Jr. and Carolyn Bessette’s relationship: A proposal.

In the show, JFK Jr. (played by Paul Kelly Anthony) pops the question during a very tense weekend with his family. Carolyn (Sarah Pidgeon) feels clearly ostracized and out of place, especially after Ethel Kennedy’s judgmental glances and she — gasp — forgets to sign up for breakfast.
John, however, doesn't seem to notice and chooses a moment while they're fishing to propose.
“The thing about fishing is, it’s always so much better with a partner,” he says. Then he takes out a silver sapphire band. He said he’d been waiting for a “perfect moment.”

Her response is warm but not conclusive. Perhaps the sting of Ethel’s grilling is still hurting. “I love you. I do want to be with you. But there’s a lot of big stuff we have to talk about before we can get married. Like, how our lives really fit together,” she says.
"Is that a no?" he responds.
"I think we need to have more of a conversation. This way I can really mean it when I say yes. Which I think is what we both want," she says.
The rest of their episode shows Carolyn inching closer to yes. They have another conversation weeks later where she expresses her concerns about becoming Mrs. JFK Jr.
"This will radically change my life," she says. John promises his personal life will be "off limits to the public" but she's skeptical. He promises to protect her.
She accepts the ring, which was his mom's, but says she'll only wear it when it's the two of them.
Somehow, though, the moment becomes the world's — it makes the tabloids' front page.
Carolyn says yes only after they have a massive fight, which leads to another breakthrough. John essentially promises he won't force her into family vacations.

"I don't want to change you. I don't want to bring you to my world. I want you to pull me out of it. I want you to be my family. You and me on our own private island even when we're in a crowded room," he says.
She stresses that she wants a simple life — not first lady. "Let's not ignore the reality of who you are and who your family is," she says.
He swears he wants to be a "good man," not a "great man," and just a good partner. That's enough.
"To be honest, I don't think marriage is necessary," she says, smoking a cigarette. "But I'm down to do it, with you."
She starts crying, realizing the weight of it. The episode ends with Carolyn crying and saying, "Yes."
"Yes, I"ll marry you. I'll take the risk with you and we'll drive each other crazy, for as long as we both shall live," she says, as The Goo Goo Dolls' "Name" plays.
What Happened in Real Life?
In real life, Carolyn Bessette-Kennedy and John F. Kennedy Jr. met in 1992 and dated briefly, before reconnecting in 1993 and getting engaged in 1995.
The show plays around with the timeline but keeps the facts the same: Bessette, indeed, wanted to keep the engagement a secret.

According to “Fairy Tale Interrupted,” the 2012 biography written by RoseMarie Terenzio, Kennedy’s assistant at George who also knew Bessette well, Kennedy proposed on the Fourth of July weekend at Martha’s Vineyard in 1995.
Kennedy took Bessette out fishing and said, "Fishing is so much better with a partner," presenting her with the same type of ring seen in the show — diamonds and sapphires.
Terenzio said Bessette told her she "didn't want anyone to know" after he proposed, according to the book.
"Carolyn was worried that when their engagement went public, the media would tear into her," Terenzio wrote.
Kennedy’s friend Robert Littell wrote in his book, “The Men We Became,” that Bessette “resisted his proposal for a year.”

Terenzio said as the summer went on, Bessette “loosened up” when it came to wearing the ring. But the Friday before Labor Day, the New York Post ran a story with a closeup of the ring, sparking rumors.
The famous fight, which was photographed and recorded, happened six months after the proposal, in February 1996.
There is no indication that Bessette finally accepted his proposal after the fight like what happens in the show.
Speaking to TODAY.com, Bessette's biographer Elizabeth Beller reminded viewers that the show is a dramatization, not a documentary.

title: Carolyn Bessette-Kennedy's Sapphire Engagement Ring Was Inspired by Jackie Onassis
summary: Since the premiere of “Love Story” on FX and Hulu, people have been celebrating Carolyn Bessette-Kennedy’s iconic style
story: Since the premiere of “Love Story” on FX and Hulu, people have been celebrating Carolyn Bessette-Kennedy’s iconic style.
The fifth episode, "Battery Park," zeroes in on a specific piece of her wardrobe: Her engagement ring.

John F. Kennedy Jr., played by Paul Kelly Anthony, proposes while the two are on an intense weekend with the entire Kennedy clan. Carolyn is unable to say yes due to various concerns, which the episode unpacks.
But she does keep coming back to the ring.
According to Elizabeth Beller, author of the Bessette biography "Once Upon a Time," the ring tied together both Kennedy's family history and Bessette's style.

Kennedy modeled the ring off of what his mother, Jacqueline Kennedy Onassis, called her “swimming ring”: “pared down and elegant, perfect for swimming.” (Her first engagement ring was a combined five carats of diamonds and emeralds.)
Kennedy Onassis’ ring was made with emerald and diamonds and set in gold, whereas Bessette’s was platinum with sapphires and diamonds.
The ring “was perfect for Carolyn and her understated (elegance)” Beller writes. The fashion executive rarely wore jewelry and was known for her simple, classic looks.
Bessette never met Kennedy’s mother, who died of cancer in 1994.

This was a point of contention for the couple, who met in 1993. Beller quoted an anonymous friend of Bessette’s who said Bessette broke up with Kennedy “a couple of times” because he wouldn’t introduce her to his mother. Once, at Keens Steakhouse, “John rushed Carolyn out a side door when he spied Jackie across the room with her companion.”

Beller quotes Kennedy’s friend Billy Way, who said Kennedy “regretted” never introducing Bessette to his mother and pointed out their similarities — both children of divorced parents, both Catholic and both with a minimalist sense of style.
Bessette and Kennedy were married in 1995 in a small, secret ceremony on Cumberland Island in Georgia.
Speaking to TODAY.com, jeweler Page Neal, co-founder of Bario Neal, calls Bessette’s ring a “study in intentional simplicity.”

“At a time when engagement rings often centered on display and visibility, hers reflected a more personal and quietly confident point of view. Its restraint is precisely what gives it lasting power,” Neal said.
The ring, Neal says, fits into Bessette’s minimal, refined and “deeply assured” style.

“She favored clean lines and understated silhouettes, outfits that never feel overworked. This ring mirrors that sensibility. An eternity band sits close to the hand so that it becomes part of the wearer, rather than something that announces itself. That subtle integration is what makes it feel so aligned with her style,” Neal says.

Unlike many modern engagement rings, this was an eternity band, meaning there was no significant center stone (usually a diamond). The idea of a “swimming ring” suggests a piece “meant to be lived in, not taken off at the first sign of activity.”
“A ring like Carolyn’s doesn’t depend on scale to feel meaningful. Its appeal is rooted in proportion, personal expression and wearability,” Neal says.

title: ‘The Traitors’ Season 4 Finale Recap: Who Won It All?
summary: Find out if the Faithfuls took home the gold, or if the Traitors stole it for themselves in "The Traitors" Season 4 finale.
story: Warning: Spoilers for “The Traitors” Season 4 finale below.
After weeks of backstabbing, tension-filled Round Tables and devious gameplay, “The Traitors” Season 4 has come to an end.

Entering the finale, which arrived on Peacock Feb. 26, only six players remained: “Love Island USA” star Rob Rausch (Traitor), singer Eric Nam (Traitor), “Dancing With the Stars” pro Mark Ballas (Faithful), “Love Island” star Maura Higgins (Faithful) and Olympic figure skaters Johnny Weir (Faithful) and Tara Lipinski (Faithful). The Traitors could see the finish line, with a pot of gold waiting for them at the end. But, they had to outmaneuver the remaining Faithfuls for one last time.

Did they make it to the Fire of Truth and win the game? Or did the Faithfuls finally work together to banish the remaining Traitors? Find out the identity of the last player (or players) standing, below.
The Final Murder

Host Alan Cumming decides to pull out all the stops for the final, public murder of the season. When the remaining players join him in front of the castle, he reveals that Mark has been murdered by the Traitors.
“See you in hell,” Mark says before lying down in a coffin.
“Slowly, death comes for us all,” Alan says as he takes a torch and lights a ring of fire, surrounding the coffin in flames. “Ashes to ashes, dust to dust.”
In his final confessional, Mark is disappointed but content with his sendoff.

“It sucks. I thought I was going to make it all the way,” he says. “But, I think I was murdered because I was kind of closing in on whoever the Traitors are and I would probably put my money on Eric right now. I’m deeply disappointed I didn’t get to take my final shot. But I did go out in a blaze of glory.”
Tara and Johnny’s Hail Mary

At breakfast, Eric and Rob both reflect on how difficult the journey to the end of the game has been. They also agree that they need to spend the day convincing Maura to vote out Johnny at the next Round Table.
Alan reminds the players that no one’s true identity will be revealed after the remaining banishments before the group starts to prepare for their last mission.

Johnny is sticking to his theory that he pitched last week: Eric and Rob are both Traitors. He pulls Tara aside and they realize that they have to bring Maura to their side or else they will be outnumbered at the next Round Table. Meanwhile, Eric and Rob are already busy smooth-talking Maura.

Tara interrupts their conversation to have a private chat with Maura. She tells her that she needs to vote for Eric to be banished next. Maura listens to her and acknowledges in her confessional that Tara’s reasoning makes sense.
The Players Make a Splash

Alan saves his most chilling and daring mission for last. The players meet him in front of the castle and he shares that all the eliminated players could be their “key to success.” He then asks for two volunteers before a helicopter flies over the castle grounds.

Rob and Maura volunteer and are given a chest that they have to hold while riding in the helicopter. Meanwhile, the rest of the players head to the loch. While seated on the helicopter, Maura tells Rob that they need to eliminate Eric next, making him worry that his plan will go awry.

The other three players steer a boat to different pontoons resting in the loch. The pontoons have questions regarding the departed players. The chest Maura and Rob hold contains key cards with all the players’ faces. They must drop the card that answers the corresponding question into the loch. Tara, Eric and Johnny then have to retrieve the card and attach it to the pontoon. If they answer all the questions correctly. They add $20,000 to the prize pot.

Both groups successfully work together and deliver the right answers. But Alan, as always, has a twist to reveal. He pauses the mission clock and says the group can double their money. Beside him is a chest with an extra $20,000. Either Maura or Rob has to jump out of the helicopter, swim to a buoy, snag the card attached to it and reach Alan before the remaining time on the clock runs out.
Rob volunteers since Maura cannot swim and completes the task, bringing the final total of the prize pot to $220,800.
The Last Round Table
Back at the castle, Johnny and Tara try, again, to persuade Maura to join their side. Maura says the game has become “messy” and she is starting to doubt herself.

When the Round Table begins, Eric speaks up for the first time in multiple episodes. He announces that he is leaning toward Johnny or Tara. They counter by saying that Eric has been quiet up until this point, which makes him look suspicious. Eric defends himself and says he has been working with and confiding in the players he trusts. Tara calls him out for throwing Maura under the bus earlier, but Rob jumps in to also share the theory that Johnny and Tara are Traitors.
The votes are split, with two for Johnny and two for Eric. Maura has the deciding vote and she banishes Johnny. Before he leaves, he gives bestie Tara a hug.

“Of course it’s not nice to lose, but I’m so proud of what I’ve done here, and I was able to do it with Tara by my side,” Johnny says in his departing confessional. “Eric and Rob are the Traitors and I hope for Tara’s sake that maybe Maura sees something that she hasn’t seen yet.”
The Fire of Truth

After the Round Table, Tara is feeling defeated. Elsewhere, Rob tells Maura she needs to eliminate Tara next. In return, Maura makes him promise that they will also eliminate Eric, who she is now fully convinced is a Traitor. Tara also makes one last ditch effort by repeatedly telling Maura that Eric and Rob are Traitors and shouldn’t be trusted.

The end is near, but first Alan chats with the players to see how they are feeling before facing the Fire of Truth. Eric is feeling nervous but has faith in his allies. Tara wants to win for herself and Johnny. Rob says all his shady moves throughout the season will be worth it if he wins. He also reveals he wants to use the money to help his family and start planning for his future family. Maura worries about putting all her trust in Rob after previously mentioning that she has been cheated on repeatedly and doesn’t trust easily.

For the Fire of Truth, each player is handed two pouches: one that says “End Game” (which is represented by green smoke) and one that says “Banish Again” (which is represented by red smoke).” The game automatically ends if only two players are remaining.
For the first vote, all the players choose to banish again. Tara voices her distrust of Eric and asks Maura to support her. But, Maura sides with Eric and Rob, again, and Tara is eliminated.

“I am feeling so devastated,” Tara says in her confessional. “When I went to Maura, I thought she realized by what I was telling her that it’s Eric and Rob. But I never in a million years thought I’d make it this far. I did the very best I could in that room and to me that’s priceless.”

During the second vote, Eric opts to end the game but Maura and Rob choose to banish again. A stunned Eric says that he is hurt that the two betrayed them. He tries to convince Maura to doubt Rob, but it doesn’t work. Maura and Rob banish Eric, with Rob telling his fellow Traitor that he had to keep the promise he made to Maura.

In his final confessional, Eric is in disbelief. “Wow. Wow.  If I’d done that,  I just wouldn’t be able to live with myself in that way,” he says about Rob’s betrayal. “Money’s great, but I think relationships are more important and it doesn’t feel good.”
Maura is smiling wide, not knowing that Rob has one more secret to confess. She proudly says she is a Faithful and waits for him to say the same.
“Maura, I am, and I always have been, a Traitor,” he reveals.

Maura is speechless and stares at Rob in silence while he repeatedly apologizes. “Oh my God. I’m so stupid. I was so fooled by you. You absolute a------. You pinky promised. You’re a piece of work,” she tells him.”
She adds, “I was so convinced. You’ve embarrassed me.”
When he tries to praise her for being the last Faithful she replies, “Don’t speak. Please stop talking.”
Maura’s final confessional begins with her just shaking her head. “Oh dear God. I’m a fool,” she laughs. “This is why I don’t trust anybody ever. Because they stab you in the g—- back.”

She tells Rob, “You’re never going to have a girlfriend after this. You’re such a good liar.” But, she isn’t bitter. “Fair play though. If I was a Traitor, I would’ve done the same. No hard feelings. I think I’m just in shock. I actually thought I was winning,” she says.
Her last words this season: “He’s a bad man. He’s a really bad, bad man. But, good for him. Rob, you’re a f------ snake. But well done.”
The Traitor Takes It All

As the last player remaining, Rob shares with Alan that he is feeling mixed emotions. “I’ve won, but I’ve also betrayed the person that I was closest to in the game,” he says before Alan points out he was playing a game called “The Traitors.”

“It’s so bittersweet,” Rob says in his confessional. “I feel bad for Maura. Poor Eric, too. But it was an opportunity that was right in front of me. Would you give $100,000 to someone you met three weeks ago? I wouldn’t.”

Alan and Rob go outside and he relishes in being the sole winner. “I could make this money go a long way. This will change my life and my family’s life. I had to think with that emotion, like I’ve done this whole game,” he shares.
As fireworks are set off behind him and he celebrates his victory, Robs marvels, “People just see me as a dumb, hot person. But, I’ve been a Traitor from day one, and I won. Beat everyone.”
What’s next for him? Well, he says he is ready to “find a nice girl that hasn’t watched TV too much,” and buy a farm where he can raise a family. “I want to have a quiet life,” he says.

title: Pink Denies She and Husband Carey Hart Separated: 'Don’t Believe the Hype'
summary: Pink is setting the record straight after reports surfaced that she and husband Carey Hart had separated.
story: Pink is setting the record straight after reports surfaced that she and husband Carey Hart had separated.

The “Cover Me in Sunshine” singer posted an Instagram video on Feb. 26 denying the rumors and writing in the caption, “Like I always say. If you don’t hear it from me, don’t believe the hype. Stay tuned though! Who knows what could happen next!?!”
In the selfie video, which contains strong language, Pink calls out the outlets that reported the separation.
“So, I was just alerted to the fact that I’m separated from my husband. I didn’t know,” she says, naming the publications and jokingly thanking them for “letting me know.”
“I was wondering, would you also like to tell our children, my 14-year-old and 9-year-old are also unaware,” she said. “Or do you want to talk about some real news?”
Instead, Pink suggested talking about the Epstein files, systemic racism or misogyny in sports, among other topics.

“Or how classy the women’s hockey team is, or how eight of the 12 medals won in the Olympics this year for the US were won by women,” she continued. “Or do you maybe want to talk about the fact that I got nominated the first year I was eligible for the Rock and Roll m------------ Hall of Fame? Do you want to talk about my accomplishments, or do you only want to talk about my supposed demise?”
Pink called the reports “fake news, not true,” before telling her followers that she loves them and “go with God.”
She ended her message by telling “trash news” to “do better.”

Pink and the former motocross racer have been married for 20 years. They first met in 2001 at the X Games and got married in 2006. Pink and Hart share two children together, daughter Willow, 14, and son Jameson, 9.
The couple, however, has broken up before. Once in 2003 before they got engaged two years later, and another time in 2008. They got back together a year later.
When asked about reconciling in a February 2013 interview with Redbook, Pink said, “People are always like, ‘Why did you get back together?’ Well, we weren’t done.”
At the time, she said that 11 months after they broke up, Hart invited her to perform at his Las Vegas club on New Year's Eve. She then gave him a photo album with cards he had given her.

“On the last page, I pasted a photo of me from a really bad movie I made years ago with my neck slit and blood everywhere,” she said. “Next to it I wrote, ‘This is me without you.’ On the next page, there was a picture of a baby. And I wrote: ‘The rest is unwritten.’ The divorce papers that we never signed were behind that page. I was like, ‘The rest is up to you.’”
She also told the Los Angeles Times in October 2023 that her song “So What” was part of what got them together.
Over the years, Pink has been candid about her 20-year marriage, telling The Guardian in 2019, “Monogamy is work! But you do the work and it’s good again.”

title: How the Ending to 'Bridgerton' Season 4 Tees up Stories for 2 Characters
summary: Find out how Season 4, Part 2 wrapped up Benedict and Sophie's Cinderella story, and learn how Francesca and Eloise's stories might unfold in Seasons 5 and 6.
story: Warning: This story contains major spoilers for the ending of "Bridgerton" Season 4 and the plots of Julia Quinn's "To Sir Phillip, With Love" and "When He Was Wicked."
Another social season has come to an end in Mayfair, and Luke Thompson's Benedict Bridgerton and Yerin Ha's Sophie Baek — sorry, Gun — have found their happily ever after.
But based on Julia Quinn's "Bridgerton" novels that inspired the hit Netflix drama, we're only halfway through the family's saga.

The illustrious Bridgerton family consists of eight alphabetically-named children, and four have yet to get their own season: Eloise (Claudia Jessie), Francesca (Hannah Dodd), Gregory (Will Tilston) and Hyacinth (Florence Hunt).

"Bridgerton" showrunner Jess Brownell previously confirmed to TODAY.com that each family member will get their own starring season of the show. So who's up next in the already renewed Seasons 5 and 6?

As Deadline reported, Brownell revealed at the red carpet for Part 1 that Eloise and Francesca will be the leads of the two upcoming seasons, though she didn't say which season goes with which character. However, some of the final moments of Season 4 are filled with clues about the storyline for next season.
But first, let's recap how Season 4, Part 2, which dropped on Netflix Feb. 26, wrapped up Benedict and Sophie's Cinderella story.
How Does 'Bridgerton' Season 4 End?
Another happy ending!
"Bridgerton" Season 4 concludes with Benedict and Sophie getting together, despite her illegitimate status and her work as a maid.

After she initially rejected his offer to be his mistress, Sophie and Benedict sleep together for the first time after he says he wants to commit to her and professes his love. Their relationship status continues to go undefined until Benedict finally wins mom Violet's (Ruth Gemmell) approval, who gives her blessing for them to quietly live together in the countryside.

But Sophie's stepmother, Araminta (Katie Leung), wants vengeance for Sophie's night out as the lady in silver. She finally tracks Sophie down and has her arrested, alleging she stole the Dowager Countess of Penwood's shoe clips.

Through a series of societal machinations, like the details of a hidden will and Violet pulling rank, the Bridgertons are able to strike a deal with Araminta. In exchange for not publicly revealing that Araminta stole the dowry willed to Sophie by her late father, Sophie will be introduced into society as a Gun and as a distant relative of the late Earl of Penwood, allowing her to be accepted by the nobility and make her an "acceptable" match for Benedict.

Queen Charlotte (Golda Rosheuvel) sweeps in at the last second to unearth all the juicy gossip involving the two families and to approve the latest Bridgerton match. Benedict later proposes publicly on the dance floor.
Who Will Lead Season 5 of 'Bridgerton'?
Season 5, which was renewed back in May 2025, will be led by either Eloise or Francesca, and the ending of "Bridgerton" Season 4 leaves potential for both stories.

Francesca, after having candid conversations with husband John, the Duke of Kilmartin (Victor Alli), finally found herself feeling more steady in their relationship. Though the surprise arrival of his cousin, Michaela (Masali Baduza), threw her off kilter again.
But Francesca and Michaela broke the ice after a party, and the trio developed a delightful friendship. However, in a shocking ending to Episode 6, Francesca finds John dead in his bed.
As she mourns, she finds herself leaning on Michaela. One night, she asks Michaela to stay in London with her amid the search for the heir to the Kilmartin line.
"If you would like it, I shall stay," Michaela says with a smile.
"I feel very close to you, Michaela," Francesca responds as she grabs Michaela's hand.
Michaela, though, looks tightly at their conjoined hands and pulls away quickly. After that slightly tense moment, Michaela suddenly departs London.
"She has packed her belongings and left," a maid tells a horrified Francesca.

As for Eloise, the most studious Bridgerton is, again, mainly stuck in the shadows this season, dodging eligible bachelors and teasing young sister Hyacinth for her dreams of the marriage mart. But by the end of the season, it's clear Eloise is starting to change her tune.
She apologizes to Hyacinth for mocking her and reveals that the death of John reminds her to "ensure that we do not allow fear to keep us from experiencing something that could be truly special."
Eloise clarifies while she’s not an outright "proponent" of marriage, she can see that "on occasion, marriage might have its advantages."

During the ball in the final episode, she finds herself in the unexpected role of matchmaker, encouraging Sophie's half-sister Posy (Isabella Wei), to dance with the handsome suitor she is eyeing. Later, Eloise smiles to herself as she sees the two talking.
What Happens in Francesca’s Book, ‘When He Was Wicked'?
The sixth book in the "Bridgerton" series, "When He Was Wicked," picks up with the events seen at the end of Season 4: John's death.
The book opens with Francesca living a life of domestic tranquility, truly in love and close friends with her husband's cousin, Michael.

After John's death, Michael disappears for a time, shirking the responsibilities of becoming the Earl of Kilmartin. When he returns, he brings a shocking confession with him: he has always been in love with Francesca.
Their story follows the push and pull between Michael and Francesca as they each navigate grief and an undeniable attraction.

In the show adaptation, Francesca is being set up for a queer spin on that tale, with Michaela replacing Michael in the story. Hints at their eventual romance were dropped as early as the Season 3 finale. Though Francesca and John were already happily married, Francesca quite literally was at a loss for words when she met Michaela for the first time, barely able to state her own name.

Michaela's romantic background, meanwhile, has not been disclosed yet in the show. But during Season 4, Episode 5, she storms out of the room after Francesca seemingly tries to set her up with a male suitor.
"I asked you kindly not to force suitors onto Michaela," John scolds Francesca after.
While they make amends, Michaela explains, "There are very few people I feel secure with, and John is one of them."
What Happens in Eloise's Book, 'To Sir Phillip, With Love'?

Like her sister, viewers have already been introduced to Eloise's love interest from the book. The novel that follows her love story is titled "To Sir Phillip, With Love," which is the fifth book in the "Bridgerton" series.

Viewers met the show's version of Phillip years ago when Season 1 debuted in 2020. Phillip (Chris Fulton) was the brother of a man that Marina (Ruby Barker), the mysterious Featherington cousin who was hiding a secret, loved. In the Season 1 finale, Marina accepted Phillip's marriage proposal and they left London together. The couple briefly appeared in Season 2, Episode 4 when Colin Bridgerton (Luke Newton) visited them.
Marina and Phillip haven't popped up on the show since then.
However, based on the book, Phillip will be re-introduced after Marina dies. He begins corresponding with Eloise, who expresses her condolences, and they later develop feelings for each other.

title: When Will ‘Formula 1: Drive to Survive’ Season 8 Premiere on Netflix and Apple TV?
summary: The Formula 1 2025 season delivered one of the most exciting seasons for motorsports fans, and "Drive to Survive" Season 8 has all the best moments. Find out how to watch.
story: The Formula 1 2025 season delivered one of the most exciting seasons for motorsports fans.
Now, people will be able to watch what went down when “Formula 1: Drive to Survive” Season 8 arrives to Netflix and, for the first time ever, Apple TV.
With Lewis Hamilton making his Ferrari debut, six rookies entering the scene and many team switch-ups, the hit sports docuseries captured all the wild and dynamic moments made for TV.

What can fans expect to see? Well, then-reigning champion Max Verstappen looks to secure his fourth consecutive win while underdog McLaren leads the season with top drivers Oscar Piastri and Lando Norris, who would ultimately become the 2025 world champion.

Elsewhere, Carlos Sainz joins Williams and leads the team to one of their best seasons in years, and Yuki Tsunoda’s performance on Racing Bulls helps him get promoted to Red Bull Racing, only to be cut from the team for the upcoming season.

There are also plenty of highs — Nico Hülkenberg emotionally securing his first-ever Formula 1 podium and the drivers attending the “F1: The Movie” premiere — and many lows — Christian Horner getting fired as Red Bull’s Team Principal. Season 8 of “Drive to Survive” will break it all down and is sure to entertain.
Read on to learn when “Formula 1: Drive to Survive” Season 8 will premiere on Netflix and now Apple TV in February.
When Does ‘Drive to Survive’ Season 8 Premiere?
“Formula 1: Drive to Survive” Season 8 will premiere on Netflix globally, Friday, Feb. 27.
For the first time ever, U.S. audiences only will also be able to stream the new season on Apple TV.
What Time Will ‘Drive to Survive’ Season 8 Be Streaming?
“Formula 1: Drive to Survive” Season 8 will stream on Netflix and Apple TV starting at 3 a.m. ET/ 12 a.m. PT.
How Many Episodes Will ‘Drive to Survive’ Season 8 Have?
“Formula 1: Drive to Survive” Season 8 will consist of eight episodes.

title: Read With Jenna's March 2026 Pick Is an 'Emotional Mystery' in a Beautiful Setting
summary: Read With Jenna's March 2026 pick is "Wait for Me" by Amy Jo Burns.
story: Jenna Bush Hager says her March Read With Jenna pick is a book as “beautiful as where it is set.”
“Wait for Me” by Amy Jo Burns unfolds partly in Appalachia, where the author grew up. Burns is from Western Pennsylvania.

“The book spans the lives of two women. We meet Elle Harlow who disappears in the ‘70s right upon her big break as a folk singer and Marijohn, who, years later, is searching for her own type of future,” Jenna says. “As these two women’s lives connect, we explore secrets long passed. “
Jenna calls it “an emotional mystery about what connects us.”
In an interview with TODAY.com, Burns said the book was inspired by a friendship she had with a woman and mentor 39 years her senior.
"Wait for Me" by Amy Jo Burns
“I wanted to write a book about the best woman that I’ve ever met. Her name was Louise,” Burns says.
“She started out as my writing teacher, but she really quickly became my friend, and she was the first person to believe in me as a writer,” she continues.
Burns went on to write the novels “Shiner,” “Mercury” and “Cinderland,” plus, of course, “Wait For Me.”
She describes Louise as someone who “had everyone’s attention” when she walked into a room. “When someone like that has faith in you, it’s contagious,” she says.
The reason “Wait for Me” took her a while to finish, Burns says, is because she thought she had to write about how was sad that Louise was gone.

“It took writing this book a million wrong ways for me to realize that what I needed, and hopefully what a reader needs out of it, is more how I felt when I was with her right and how unstoppable she made me,” she says.
“She brought out the best version of myself. I just wanted to celebrate what it’s like to welcome like a meteoric woman like that into your life, and how it shapes everything,” she says.
Burns learned she was a Read With Jenna pick while at the school bus stop with her kids, the time of day that’s “most hectic for a mom.”

“It felt really perfect to me, because I used to think that the only way you could become a writer was that you would go off into the woods for months at a time, until you had an epiphany, and then you wrote this book and came back,” she says.

“My life has never been that way. My life has always been writing in the context of building a life and building a family. So the fact that my kids were there, even though I couldn’t tell them, it just it felt very earned and perfect.”

While reading, she hopes readers ask themselves, “What thing in life would I really go for if I wasn’t afraid? I think all the characters are really wrestling with that. I also hope a reader would ask themselves about the obstacles in life they’ve overcome that they never gave themselves credit for.”

title: BTS Fans Can Watch Their 'Arirang Tour' In Theaters. Here's When
summary: BTS have big plans for their highly anticipated comeback — including watching their concerts on the big screen
story: BTS have big plans for their highly anticipated comeback — including watching their concerts on the big screen.
After all seven members officially completed their mandatory military services, the K-pop superstars are gearing up to release a new album and go on tour.

BTS — which consists of RM, Jin, Suga, J-Hope, Jimin, V and Jung Kook — will release their new album, “Airiang” on March 20. They will then head on the road for their massive world tour titled “BTS World Tour Arirang” that kicks off April 9 in South Korea.
On Feb. 25, they announced that at least two of their concerts —April 11 from Goyang and April 18 from Tokyo — will be shown in theaters.
“Experience the first two stops of the BTS World Tour ‘Arirang’ live in cinemas worldwide,” reads the official tweet, which also notes that “more live viewings to be announced at a later date.”

At this time, fans can secure tickets for showings of their first two dates. Their website also details which countries will be able to have live, delayed live or encore presentations of the concerts, depending on the time zone.

“Featuring a spectacular 360-degree in-the-round stage design, the show delivers an immersive experience that places audiences at the very center of the performance,” reads the event’s description. “Share the electrifying moments of BTS’s defining cultural comeback together on the big screen worldwide, April 11 from Goyang and April 18 from Tokyo.”

This marks the group’s first large-scale tour in four years. Ahead of their comeback and to celebrate the release of their fifth studio album, BTS will take the stage for a special concert, “BTS The Comeback Live| Arirang,” in Gwanghwamun Square in Seoul, South Korea, on March 21 that will stream on Netflix.
And that’s not all. The group will also release a documentary, “BTS: The Return,” on Netflix March 27, which will showcase their return and making of their album.

title: Is There an Accent Connor Storrie Can’t Do? Apparently Not According to His ‘SNL’ Promo
summary: "Heated Rivalry" star Connor Storrie proves his talents are limitless and impresses with his accents in his “Saturday Night Live" promo.
story: Rising star Connor Storrie is proving that his talents are limitless in the latest funny promo for “Saturday Night Live.”

Ahead of his debut as host of the NBC sketch-comedy series on Saturday, Feb. 28, Storrie gave viewers a taste of what they can expect to see when he takes over Studio 8H. In a promo released on Feb. 25, the “Heated Rivalry” star, who impressed fans with his portrayal of Russian hockey player Ilya Rozanov in the steamy drama, confirmed that there isn’t an accent he can’t master.
The clip begins with “SNL” cast members Marcello Hernández and Ashley Padilla complimenting Storrie on his Russian accent in the show. Storrie replies, “I actually do all kinds of accents.”

That’s when “SNL” cast member James Austin Johnson enters the room. The comedian, who delights viewers with his spot-on impressions, walks in wearing a sheriff costume and speaking with a Southern twang. “I reckon this here show ain’t big enough for the both of us,” he challenges.
Storrie responds in an equally impressive Southern accent, launching an accent duel.

Hernández and Padilla start calling out different countries and ethnic groups to see if Storrie and Johnson can impersonate a local. They also throw in the Na’vi species from the “Avatar” franchise and “Shrek.”
For his final accent, Storrie transforms into his “Heated Rivalry” character.
“When you get to hell, tell them Rozanov sent you,” he says before pretending to shoot Johnson, defeating him.
Fans will get to see more of Storrie’s comedic range when he leads the next new episode of “SNL,” which features Mumford & Sons as the musical guest.
Speaking about his preparation for his big night, Storrie told “Access Hollywood,” “I think this is the coolest thing I’ve ever done in my entire life, literally.”
He added, “So, I think there’s a certain amount of nerves that come with it. But I think if you can use that energy, you’re kind of in the best case.”

title: 'Survivor 50' Cast: The 24 Returning Players Include Legends Like Cirie Fields and Ozzy Lusth
summary: Who is in the cast of Survivor 50? See the cast members' bios, names and what season of Survivor they were cast on.
story: “Survivor” fans, are you ready? After many years — in some cases decades — of waiting to watch your favorite castaways compete again, the time has finally come to see some of the biggest icons battle it out on the historic “Survivor 50.”
On Wednesday, Feb. 25, 24 castaways will make their grand return to our television screens, all with the goal of outwitting, outlasting and outplaying each other for the title of “Sole Survivor.”

In the epic three-hour premiere, viewers will be reintroduced to legends of the game, like Cirie Fields and Ozzy Lusth, before the scheming begins. "White Lotus" creator Mike White is returning to Fiji, too.

Fans have been anticipating this moment since host Jeff Probst revealed the 24 players who have the honor of competing on Season 50 in May 2025. At the time, Probst only shared 22 names, with the remaining competitors coming from the then-yet-to-air Season 49. When that season wrapped in December, the final two players were confirmed: Rizo Velovic and Season 49 winner Savannah Louie.

Not only will Velovic and Louie have the additional challenge of playing back-to-back, but they also have to face fan-favorites, villains and past winners who have been eagerly hoping for another chance to win.
So, who else makes up the all-returning cast? Get to know the castaways of “Survivor 50,” and their original seasons, below.
Season 50 ‘Survivor’ Contestants
Jenna Lewis-Dougherty
Past “Survivor” appearances: Seasons 1 (Borneo), 8 (All-Stars)
Colby Donaldson
Past “Survivor” appearances: Seasons 2 (The Australian Outback), 8 (All-Stars), 20 (Heroes vs. Villains)
Stephenie LaGrossa Kendrick
Past “Survivor” appearances: Seasons 10 (Palau), 11 (Guatemala), 20 (Heroes vs. Villains)
Cirie Fields
Past “Survivor” appearances: Seasons 12 (Panama-Exile Island), 16 (Micronesia — Fans vs. Favorites), 20 (Heroes vs. Villains), 34 (Game Changers)
Ozzy Lusth
Past “Survivor” appearances: Seasons 13 (Cook Islands), 16 (Micronesia — Fans vs. Favorites), 23 (South Pacific), 34 (Game Changers)
Benjamin “Coach” Wade
Past “Survivor” appearances: Seasons 18 (Tocantins — The Brazilian Highlands), 20 (Heroes vs. Villains), 23 (South Pacific)
Aubry Bracco
Past “Survivor” appearances: Seasons 32 (Kaôh Rōng — Brains vs. Brawn vs. Beauty), 34 (Game Changers), 38 (Edge of Extinction)
Chrissy Hofbeck
Past “Survivor” appearance: Season 35 (Heroes vs. Healers vs. Hustlers)
Christian Hubicki
Past “Survivor” appearance: Season 37 (David vs. Goliath)
Angelina Keeley
Past “Survivor” appearance: Season 37 (David vs. Goliath)
Mike White
Past “Survivor” appearance: Season 37 (David vs. Goliath)
Rick Devens
Past “Survivor” appearance: Season 38 (Edge of Extinction)
Jonathan Young
Past “Survivor” appearance: Season 42
Dee Valladares
Past “Survivor” appearance: Season 45 winner
Emily Flippen
Past “Survivor” appearance: Season 45
Quintavius ‘Q’ Burdette
Past “Survivor” appearance: Season 46
Tiffany Ervin
Past “Survivor” appearance: Season 46
Charlie Davis
Past “Survivor” appearance: Season 46
Genevieve Mushaluk
Past “Survivor” appearance: Season 47
Kamilla Karthigesu
Past “Survivor” appearance: Season 48
Kyle Fraser
Past “Survivor” appearance: Season 48 winner
Joseph Hunter
Past “Survivor” appearance: Season 48
Rizo Velovic
Past “Survivor” appearance: Season 49
Savannah Louie
Past “Survivor” appearance: Season 49 winner
What to know about Season 50

Season 50 of “Survivor” will “push the series into uncharted territory and impact the players like never before” since the game is now “In the Hands of the Fans,” the season’s subtitle, for the first time, according to a previous press release.

Leading up to the season, fans voted on several elements of the game, including the following: “Idols or No Idols,” “Final Four Fire Making: Keep It or Lose It” and “Live Finale and Reunion Show in L.A. — or Keep the Winner Reveal and Aftershow in the Jungles of Fiji.”

To keep an element of surprise, contestants won’t know what fans voted for until they begin the competition. But, teasers for the season have confirmed that idols, including special versions like the Billie Eilish Idol, will be in play, and that celebrities like Jimmy Fallon and Zac Brown will also impact the game.

title: What Hilary Duff Reveals About Estrangement From Sister Haylie on Song 'We Don't Talk': Lyrics, Explained
summary: Hilary Duff's long-rumored estrangement from her sister, Haylie Duff, was confirmed by one of the songs on her new album, "Luck... or Something."When the "Lizzie McGuire" star debuted the song "We Don't Talk," fans instantly wondered if the lyrics offered a glimpse into the status of her relationship with her sister.
story: Hilary Duff's long-rumored estrangement from her sister, Haylie Duff, was confirmed by one of the songs on her new album, "Luck... or Something."

When the "Lizzie McGuire" star debuted the song "We Don't Talk," fans instantly wondered if the lyrics offered a glimpse into the status of her relationship with her sister. Now, the singer has confirmed the song's subject.
Read on for some of the notable lyrics to "We Don't Talk" and what Hilary Duff has said about what it means.
'We Don't Talk' Lyrics, Explained
In the first verse of "We Don't Talk," Hilary Duff references a strained relationship.
"I’m not sure when it happened/ Not even sure what it was about/ If I did something different/ Would you feel something different?/ Would you at least let me hear you out?" she sings.
The lyrics go on to imply that the singer is talking about a relative.

"‘Cause we come from the same home, the same blood/ A different combination, but the same lock/ People ask me how you’re doing/ I wanna say, 'Amazing'/But the truth is that I don’t know/ What I always end up sayin’ is how/ We don’t talk," she sings.
In the second verse, Hilary Duff talks about the experience of undergoing an "emotional eviction" and lacking a "sentimental overlap" with the song's subject.
"And if it’s ‘cause you’re jealous/ God knows I would sell it all/ Then break you off the bigger half," she sings.
Before getting into the chorus, Hilary Duff sings, "People ask me if I’ve seen you/ And honestly, I hate it/ ‘Cause the truth is that I need to/ But there’s no way to relay it."
But toward the end of the song, Hilary Duff expresses hope for a reconciliation.

"Let’s have it out/ I’ll hear you out/ You hear me out/ On the couch/ Get back to how we were as kids/ Break it down/ So sick of being/ So sad about/ How we don’t talk/ And you won’t talk about it," she sings.
Who Is Haylie Duff?
Haylie Duff, 41, is the older sister of Hilary Duff.
Similar to her younger sister, Haylie Duff rose to fame in the early 2000s and starred in several notable projects, including "Napoleon Dynamite" and "7th Heaven."
Haylie Duff also made guest appearances in several TV series during the early days of career, including "Chicago Hope," "Boston Public" and "That's So Raven."

The Duff sisters worked together on several occasions. Haylie Duff appeared in three episodes of "Lizzie McGuire" and was the singing voice of Isabella Parigi in "The Lizzie McGuire Movie," per her IMDB page.

In 2006, the sisters starred alongside each other in the film "Material Girls." They also recorded a cover of the song "Our Lips Are Sealed" for the soundtrack of Hilary Duff's hit film, "A Cinderella Story."
In recent years, Haylie Duff has worked as a director on several projects for Lifetime and Tubi.
Who Is 'We Don't Talk' About?
In an interview with CBS Mornings, Hilary Duff confirmed that "We Don't Talk" is about her sister.
"It’s definitely about my sister. And just absolutely the most lonely part of my existence is not having my sister in my life at the moment," she said.
Hilary Duff admitted that she "really struggled" with whether or not to include the song on her record.

"But it’s funny, as a person that exists in the world without my like other half, so many people are having that experience. And a lot of conversations I have with people are like, 'Yeah, me too. Me too. Me too,'" she said.
Ultimately, Hilary Duff decided to put the song on her record because she said it's her "truth."
While appearing on the "Call Her Daddy" podcast, Hilary Duff also addressed the attention her song has received.

"People have known everybody in my life since I was on 'Lizzie McGuire.' They know my mom, my sister, my dad. Like, we have a small family. People are interested in that for some reason. And it’s really sad, you know, kind of like where we are right now and scary to share it because of the internet, but important for me. I’m just saying how it feels for me," she said.
Hilary Duff said she intentionally tried to avoid "placing blame" on anyone on her album.

"1t takes everybody involved, you know, for things to kind of be where they are. But I was just trying to talk about how it feels like for me and reach people because it’s real and a lot of people share a similar story," she said.
When asked if she was hoping to reconcile with her sister, Hilary Duff said she made the album for herself.
"My purpose for making my record was for myself and I think that, like, you always hope for something to be healthy again, you know, and something to be good again," she said.
However, the singer said she's not sure if she will be "met with openness or anger."
"There’s many different options there. And I think I had to just let go of an expectation and do what I wanted for me," she said. "And that’s that, you know, it’s a painful thing to talk about."

title: 'Real Housewives of Salt Lake City' Star Mary Cosby's Son, Robert, Dies at 23
summary: Mary Cosby's son, Robert Cosby Jr., has died, police confirmed on Feb. 25.
story: Mary Cosby's son, Robert Cosby Jr., has died, police confirmed on Feb. 25. He was 23.

The Salt Lake City Police Department said officers responded to a "possible overdose" on Feb. 23, and on the scene, they found Robert Cosby Jr. dead. The investigation remains open as the medical examiner works to determine a cause of death.

“Real Housewives of Salt Lake City” star Mary Cosby and her husband Robert Cosby Sr. expressed their heartache in a statement, saying, “Our beloved son Robert Jr. has been called home to the Lord. Though our hearts ache, we take comfort in God’s promise and in knowing he is finally at peace. We are grateful for your prayers and trust in the Lord to carry us through this time of sorrow.”

Cosby Jr.'s death comes after public health and legal troubles. In a November 2024 episode of his mother’s reality show, the then 22-year-old sat down with her while the cameras rolled and opened up about his drug use, which he said included Adderall, Xanax, cocaine, acid and molly.
Andy Cohen called it “one of the most raw, real and heart-wrenching” moments in the Bravo franchise’s history during an November 2024 episode of “Watch What Happens Live.”

Mourning the death of their son, the Cosbys added in an additional statement, “His untimely passing is hard to comprehend, and the Cosby’s are struggling to understand how someone so young and full of life could be gone so soon.”

“Robert Jr.’s warm spirit, humor and kind heart will be missed,” the statement continued. “A favorite bible passage of the Cosby family is Ecclesiastes 1:2-8, which touches upon the idea that human pursuits, pleasures and accomplishments are fleeting and futile without spiritual purpose. This loss is sudden, and the family asks for privacy during this difficult time.”
As the news broke, Cohen shared his condolences on Threads.
“Devastatingly sad news out of SLC,” he wrote. “This is every parent’s worst nightmare. My heart is broken for Mary, and I am sending all my love to her and Robert Sr.”

Bravo, which airs "Real Housewives of Salt Lake City," said in a statement, “We are heartbroken to learn of the passing of Mary’s beloved son, Robert Jr. Mary is a cherished member of our family, and our thoughts, love, and deepest condolences are with her and her loved ones during this incredibly difficult time.”
Mary Cosby told TODAY.com in January 2025 that she considered herself a “helicopter mom” who wanted to protect her only child “at all costs.”
Cosby said that her son had completed rehab after the “Real Housewives of Salt Lake City” episode aired.
“I can’t control anyone, including my child’s path or their journey,” she told TODAY.com. “I have to learn from it, and I have to let it be and let him learn from it on its own.”

Robert Cosby Jr. was later arrested in September 2025 for “a misdemeanor domestic violence matter” involving his then-wife, Alexiana Smokoff, according to court documents obtained by NBC News. He faced charges related to violating orders to stay away from Smokoff.

In December 2025, he pleaded guilty to assault and violating a protective order, per NBC News. He had faced more than a dozen charges stemming from incidents from September to November 2025 that were related to violations of orders to stay away from Smokoff.
His attorney moved at a hearing for pretrial release, but the court denied the motion, which meant Cosby remained in jail until his sentencing on Feb. 3.
Robert Cosby Jr.’s lawyer, Clayton Simms, told People in statements published Feb. 4 that the 23-year-old was released from jail after two months.
Mary Cosby addressed her son’s arrest during the Season 6 reunion of “Salt Lake City,” which aired Jan. 20. Through tears, she confirmed her son was in jail and that Smokoff was now his ex-wife.

“I’m not worried about him. I know he’s somewhere where he’s not using,” she said. “At some point I have to step back so that he can learn. And he can make his own decisions, and unfortunately he’s learning the hard way.”

If you or someone you know is struggling with an alcohol, drug or other substance abuse problem, call the free and confidential helpline of the Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration at 1-800-662-HELP (1-800-662-4357), or visit findtreatment.gov.

title: 'Scrubs' Reboot Cast: Which Original Stars Are Returning?
summary: Who is in the cast of the new Scrubs reboot? Who is joining? Who is leaving? See a list of the original cast members.
story: The highly anticipated “Scrubs” reboot is finally here.

It’s been 16 years since the medical comedy aired its final episode in 2010. Now, many familiar faces from the show are back in action. The season is poised to pick up after the show’s eighth season, not the ninth, which was not received well by fans.
The nine-episode revival series will premiere on ABC at 8 p.m. ET/7 p.m. CT on Feb. 25. Episodes will also stream on Hulu the day after they air.
Curious to see who’s returning for the reboot and what new faces are joining? Here’s everything you need to know about the cast.
Returning Cast Members

Zach Braff was already down for participating in a “Scrubs” reboot long before it became a reality. While speaking with TODAY’s Hoda Kotb and Jenna Bush Hager in 2023, Braff was optimistic about the possibility of a revival series.
“There’s a lot of talk about it at this very moment. We’ll see, you know. If the stars were to align I’m very open to it,” he said.
Braff will reprise his role as John “J.D.” Dorian, a doctor at Sacred Heart Hospital.
In an interview with E! News, Braff described what viewers can expect from the reboot.
“There’s drama, there’s comedy, there’s these wacky fantasies. But the baseline is that the show is based in reality,” he said.
Donald Faison, is stepping back into the role of Dr. Christopher Turk. In the original series, Turk started as a surgical intern and later became chief of surgery, per NBC.
Alongside Braff, Faison has revisited the original series in a podcast titled “Fake Doctors, Real Friends with Zach and Donald.”
In an interview with The Hollywood Reporter, Faison said the podcast helped him and Braff conduct research before the reboot.

“We really realized what we liked; the fans told us what they liked, told us what they didn’t like. We were very honest with ourselves about the things we were watching and how we were performing. Season 9 was like, ‘What the heck were we doing?’” he said.
Faison said the original series “kind of got off the rails” towards the end, but said the revival brings everything “back to reality and grounded the show quite a bit.”
In the reboot series, Sarah Chalke returns to the role of Dr. Elliot Reid. While speaking with E! News, Chalke said it was a no-brainer for her to join the reboot.
“What did I need to come back? Um, to be asked,” she teased.
The actor also explained something that was important to her before signing on.

“And also just obviously that we would all come back together. I think that was something for all of us that was really important because we talked about this for a couple of years. But we’d all said like, ‘Well, if we’re going to do it, we all want to do it,’” she said.
Judy Reyes was known as nurse Carla during her time on “Scrubs.”
According to Deadline, the actor left the show at the end of Season 8. In the show, Carla’s exit was tied to her desire to take care of her children.
Reyes reprises her role in four episodes of the revival, per Deadline. While speaking about her experience on set, she described the dynamic with her co-stars.

“The show is a true ensemble, so we’re always together, our days are long and the work is really hard, but when it’s there, the words are easy to remember, the scenes are fun to play, and the actors are great to be with, and everybody who works on the show is happy to be there,” she said.
Similar to Reyes, John C. McGinley will be reprising his role as Dr. Perry Cox in a recurring capacity. Per Deadline, he will appear in three episodes of the reboot.
When asked if they would return for a second season of the revival, Reyes said, “F--- yeah.”
McGinley replied, saying, “I echo that one. F--- yeah, that’s as good as it gets.”
In a separate interview with Deadline, McGinley described his reaction to being asked to return for the reboot.

“It was the single biggest no brainer of all time because it had been an active rumor for about two and a half years. And then when it happened, for all of us it felt like, ‘Finally.’ And I don’t say that out of arrogance. It just felt like, ‘Yeah!’” he said.
Neil Flynn, who is known for his roles in “The Middle” and “Mean Girls,” appeared in 170 episodes of “Scrubs” as the janitor.
Showrunner Aseem Batra recently told TVLine that Flynn will return for one episode of the reboot.
Flynn appears in a promotional video for the series. In the brief clip, someone drops a coffee cup and Flynn says, “You know, I’m not cleaning that up.”
Christa Miller appeared in 90 episodes of “Scrubs” as Jordan Sullivan.
In an interview with TVLine, Batra also confirmed that Miller will make an appearance in one episode of the reboot.
Miller appears in a promotional video for the series that features the following caption: “She’s back and better than ever.”

While teasing Miller’s storyline, Batra offered a sneak peek at what fans can expect, saying, “All I can say is that it’s stuff that we all deal with as we get older. And it’s something that, hopefully, when we talk about the dynamic between J.D. and Dr. Cox, it gives us even more rich places to go with those two characters.”
Robert Maschio appeared in 130 episodes of “Scrubs” as Todd Quinlan. Variety recently reported that the actor was returning for the reboot. It’s unclear how many episodes he will appear in.
Phill Lewis, who appeared in six episodes of “Scrubs” as Hooch, will be seen in the reboot, per Variety. At this time, it’s uncertain how many episodes he will appear in.
New Cast Members

The Hollywood Reporter recently revealed that Vanessa Bayer would be joining the cast as Sibby, “who is in HR or mental health or some amalgamation of elements that let her be a general wet blanket, restricting some of the same old behaviors — Todd’s sexism, Cox’s abuse, J.D.’s everything — as if to suggest that society hasn’t changed, but one smiling administrator has.”
Bayer is known for her roles in “Trainwreck” and “Office Christmas Party” and has appeared in 150 episodes of “Saturday Night Live.”
Joel Kim Booster is known for his roles in “Fire Island” and “The Week Of.” The actor will star as Dr. Eric Park in the reboot.

The Hollywood Reporter describes his character as “a surgeon who immediately becomes J.D.’s adversary, even though Dr. Park is correct about absolutely everything and J.D. is wrong about absolutely everything.”
Ava Bunn is known for her roles in “Head Shot” and “Worked to Death.” She will star as Sam Tosh in the “Scrubs” revival.
Per TV Insider, Bunn plays a medical intern who is also an influencer.

“She paid for her medical school through the money she made off social media, which I feel like is prevalent in today’s times. You can low-key make bank off social media. So, highlighting that throughout the season, you watch her grow and try to earn the respect of her fellow doctors, and she’s fun and sassy,” she told the publication.
Jacob Dudman is known for roles in “The Choral” and “The Stranger.” In the “Scrubs” reboot, he plays intern Asher Green.
“He is an intern with an excellent bedside manner, but he has little to no confidence in his abilities, both medically and socially,” he told TV Insider.
David Gridley is known for guest appearances on “High Potential” and “The Rookie.” In the “Scrubs” reboot, he will play medical intern Blake Lewis.
“Blake is this alpha male sort of character with this maverick mentality,” he told TV Insider. “He doesn’t really want to integrate himself with the other medical interns.”

Layla Mohammadi is known for her roles in “The Persian Version” and “Lioness.” The actor will star as Amara Hadi in the “Scrubs” reboot. Mohammadi told TV Insider her character “grew up very sheltered,” and in the new show she comes face-to-face with the “messiness of life.”
Amanda Morrow has appeared in one episode of “Law & Order: Special Victims Unit” and is a part of the cast of “The Devil Wears Prada 2.”
In the “Scrubs” reboot, she will play surgical intern Dashana Trainor. In an interview with TV Insider, she described her character.

“You’re going to watch Dashna come up against herself, become a fiercely loyal friend, and become more and more confident in her abilities as she is when you meet her. She grows in her mentorship with Dr. Turk,” Morrow said.

title: When Does 'Survivor' Season 50 Start? How to Watch
summary: Survivor 50 premieres tonight, February 25, on CBS. Here's what time it starts, how to watch and stream and what else to know.
story: "Survivor" is celebrating a major achievement with its latest season.
It's been nearly 26 years since the popular reality competition first premiered in May 2000. Now, the series is heading into Season 50 and promising an epic adventure for viewers.
Before "Survivor 50" premieres, we're sharing everything you need to know about the highly anticipated milestone season.
When Does 'Survivor 50' Premiere?
"Survivor" Season 50 premieres on Wednesday, Feb. 25, 2026. The three-hour season premiere will air on CBS between 8 to 11 p.m. ET/PT.
How to Watch 'Survivor 50'
Viewers can tune in for new episodes of Season 50 on CBS. Additionally, the series is available to stream on Paramount+.
Paramount+ Premium subscribers can watch the show live and on demand and Paramount+ Essential subscribers can watch it on demand the day after each episode airs.
What to Expect from 'Survivor 50'
"Survivor" host and executive producer Jeff Probst will return once again for the milestone season, which takes place on the islands of Fiji.
Per a press release, the theme of Season 50 is “In the Hands of the Fans” and will feature “decisions from the fans” that will impact that game.
Who Is in the Cast of 'Survivor' Season 50?
The season will feature 24 returning contestants who are competing for the $1 million prize. Here's the full list:

title: Trump says 'a large amount' of Iran's leadership is gone
summary: President Donald Trump said Saturday that U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran killed Ayatollah Ali Khamenei and other top leaders.
story: President Donald Trump said Saturday that U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran killed Ayatollah Ali Khamenei and other top leaders.

“The people that make all the decisions, most of them are gone,” Trump told NBC News in a phone call, adding that “a large amount of leadership” in Iran was also killed, although he declined to go into more detail.
Shortly after the call, Trump posted on Truth Social that Khamenei had been killed in U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran.

“Khamenei, one of the most evil people in History, is dead,” he wrote. “This is not only Justice for the people of Iran, but for all Great Americans, and those people from many Countries throughout the World, that have been killed or mutilated by Khamenei and his gang of bloodthirsty THUGS.”
Iranian state television network Press TV confirmed Khamenei's death on social media.
"Leader of Islamic Revolution Ayatollah Ali Khamenei martyred in US-Israeli attacks on Iran," it posted on X.

Iranian state media outlet IRNA said later that Secretary of the Iranian Defense Council Ali Shamkhani and Mohammad Pakpour, commander of the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps, were killed in the airstrikes. Previously, the Israel Defense Forces said the two were among those killed.

NBC News asked Trump who will next lead Iran. He replied, “I don’t know, but at some point they’ll be calling me to ask who I’d like,” adding that he was “only being a little sarcastic when I say that.”

“But look, it’s going very well. It’s been, you know, we’re, we’re probably, in terms of zero-to-10, we’re close to the 10 spot, if not there, and it’s thus far been going well, but we have a ways to go. Tremendous damage has been inflicted. The leadership is gone,” he said. “Large, large portions of the leadership.”

Trump, who has been monitoring the U.S.-Israel joint operations from his Mar-a-Lago estate in Florida, announced “major combat operations” in Iran in a video statement on his Truth Social account early Saturday.

“Our objective is to defend the American people by eliminating imminent threats from the Iranian regime, a vicious group of very hard, terrible people. Its menacing activities directly endanger the United States, our troops, our bases overseas and our allies throughout the world,” he said, while also warning that the strikes could result in casualties among Americans, including deaths.
He also urged Iranian citizens to take over their government, saying: “It will be yours to take. This will be probably your only chance for generations.”

The strikes, part of Operation Epic Fury, began at 1:15 a.m. ET, according to U.S. Central Command. “Targets included Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps command and control facilities, Iranian air defense capabilities, missile and drone launch sites, and military airfields,” CENTCOM said.
The strikes were launched from the air, land and sea, it added, and the U.S. also used “low-cost one-way attack drones for the first time in combat.”
Talks over Iran’s missile and nuclear capabilities failed to yield a diplomatic agreement this week.

Most Republican members of Congress lauded the strikes, but Democrats, along with Rep. Thomas Massie and Sen. Rand Paul, both Kentucky Republicans, expressed outrage that Trump acted unilaterally without congressional approval. Some of the critical lawmakers said Saturday that they plan to force a war powers vote in the House next week.

Asked by NBC News how he’ll know whether the operation is done and whether it was a success, Trump said: “I think it’s already a success. We’ve inflicted tremendous damage. It would take them years to rebuild.”

title: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, religious cleric who ruled Iran for decades, killed in strikes
summary: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who reigned as Iran’s supreme leader for the past 36 years, was killed in a sweeping U.S. and Israeli attack on the country Saturday.
story: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who reigned as Iran’s supreme leader for the past 36 years, was killed in a sweeping U.S. and Israeli attack on the country Saturday. He was 86.
President Donald Trump announced his death on social media and state TV later confirmed it.

To Khamenei’s critics, he was a despot who consolidated power over nearly four decades, bolstered the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corp to become the most powerful military and economic force in the country, and clung to power in his final days with a brutal crackdown on anti-government protesters that killed thousands.
To his supporters, he will be revered as a martyr who stood up to the aggression of the U.S. and Israel, which he said should be wiped off the map.

His death leaves a nation that he ruled with an iron grip for decades unmoored, possibly heading to a period of great turmoil.  Two opposing factions, the protesters pushing for greater freedom and the heavily armed security forces confronting them, are now vying for power while American and Israeli bombs continue to fall.
“Khamenei, one of the most evil people in History, is dead,” Trump posted Saturday on Truth Social.

title: Iran's foreign minister says he's unsure why U.S. attacked during nuclear talks
summary: Iran's foreign minister says he's unsure why U.S. attacked during nuclear talks
story: Changing the Iranian regime is “mission impossible,” the country’s foreign minister told NBC News, hours after the U.S. and Israel launched a major attack on the Islamic Republic and President Donald Trump called on its citizens to overthrow their leaders.
“You cannot do regime change while millions of people are supporting the so-called regime,” Abbas Araghchi said in an interview from the capital Tehran.

On Thursday, a team of Iranian negotiators were talking with U.S. special envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner, Trump’s son-in-law, in Geneva with the aim of averting a potential military assault, “and a deal was at our reach,” Araghchi said.

“We were able to address serious questions related to Iran’s nuclear program. We obviously have differences, but we resolved some of those differences, and we decided to continue in order to resolve the rest of questions,” he said, adding he didn’t know why while the talks were progressing “they decided to attack us.”
Other senior officials in the regime had survived, Araghchi said, including President Masoud Pezeshkian, the head of the judiciary and the parliament speaker. Two commanders were killed.
He also said that “as far as I know,” the supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, is alive, although Trump later said Iranian state media later confirmed it.

The strikes, during the holy Muslim fasting month of Ramadan, came weeks after a U.S. military operation that captured Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro and his wife, Cilia Flores, and brought them to New York to face federal drug conspiracy charges.
It also marked the second time in eight months that the Trump administration has used military force against the Islamic Republic.

In a video announcing the “major combat operations,” Trump told Iranians to “take over your government” when the U.S. was finished. “It will be yours to take,” he said. “This will be probably your only chance for generations.”
His comments were echoed by Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, who said the operation would “create the conditions for the brave Iranian people to take their fate into their own hands.”
But Araghchi said it was not possible to spark regime change because Iran’s government was “supported by the people.”
Unprecedented nationwide unrest last month saw authorities in Iran launch a deadly crackdown.

The U.S.-based Human Rights Activists News Agency has said it confirmed more than 7,000 deaths and that it is investigating thousands more. The group says that it verifies each death with a network of activists on the ground in Iran and that its data goes through “multiple internal checks.”
Iran’s government has acknowledged more than 3,000 people were killed.
“Yes, there are also people who are complaining, but there are strong supporters of the regime,” said Araghchi. “And then we have a very well-established political structure.”
Millions of people came out onto the streets in cities across the country to mark the recent anniversary of the 1979 revolution, Araghchi noted.
He added that the U.S. and others had tried and failed to do this in the past, so if they want to repeat a failed experience, “they won’t get any better result.”
While there was “no communication right now” with the U.S., Araghchi said, Tehran was interested in de-escalation and ready to talk once the joint U.S.-Israeli strikes end.

Iran was “certainly interested for de-escalation,” and American negotiators could contact him if they wanted to resume talks, he said. “This is a war of choice by the United States, and they have to pay for that,” he added. “But as far as we are concerned, we don’t want war.”

Disputing Trump’s claim in his State of the Union address that the Islamic Republic is building missiles capable of hitting the U.S., Araghchi said Iran had no desire to do so and had intentionally restricted the range of its missiles.

“We don’t want to do that because we don’t have any hostility against, you know, the United States people,” he said. Iran, he added, had built weapons “in order to defend ourselves against our enemies.”

American forces were attacking our people in our cities, he said, “but this is not what we are going to do. We are attacking the Americans bases, military bases in the region, and military installations and facilities, and this is only as an act of self-defense.”
He also referenced what Iran says was a deadly strike on a school in the southern city of Minab. Dozens were killed in the incident, local officials have said.

In an earlier post on X, he shared a photo of dozens of people surrounding a heavily damaged building with smoke rising from the facility, which he said was “bombed in broad daylight, when packed with young pupils.”
